title: hbr-matrix-check.mjs gov-check sub-check (build-time gate)
goal: Build the `hbr-matrix-check.mjs` sub-check that `just gov-check` invokes at build time to verify every Applicable HBR row in a packet's PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX has a non-null evidence_pointer and a validator_verdict in {PROVED, NOT_APPLICABLE}. PENDING/STEER/BLOCKED rows block the gov-check pass; failures emit machine-readable failure rows with hbr_id + reason.
expected_diff_shape: New script .GOV/roles_shared/checks/hbr-matrix-check.mjs; justfile recipe addition wiring the sub-check into the gov-check umbrella; test fixture exercising pass/fail paths.
unblock_conditions: MT-001 loader + MT-002 hydrator merged; packets carry hydrated matrix rows.
implementation_notes: Per spec §5.6.2 (AC-HBR-HANDOFF-GATE) HBR gate evaluation runs HARD at two points: (a) build time via `just gov-check`, (b) handoff time on every governed inter-role transition. This MT covers point (a). The check accepts CLI args `--packet <path>` (single) or `--all-packets` (walk .GOV/task_packets/*/packet.json). For each packet: read `acceptance_matrix.hbr` array, group rows by status, and FAIL the check if any row has status in {PENDING, STEER, BLOCKED}. PROVED rows must additionally carry a non-empty `evidence_pointer` (string path or receipt-id reference) and `validator_verdict=="PROVED"`. NOT_APPLICABLE rows are only allowed if they appear in `acceptance_matrix.hbr_not_applicable` with a captured reason — a NOT_APPLICABLE in the hbr array without a parallel ledger entry is itself a fail. Exit codes: 0=pass, 2=fail (matrix violations), 3=error (malformed input). Stderr output is JSONL: one `{hbr_id, packet, reason, severity}` per failure. Justfile recipe: add `hbr-matrix-check` recipe that runs `node .GOV/roles_shared/checks/hbr-matrix-check.mjs --all-packets`, and wire it into the umbrella `gov-check` recipe (find it in justfile; if absent, the umbrella may be implied — add a comment marking the new sub-check). Tests: golden fixture packet with one PROVED row passes; same fixture with one PENDING row fails with exit 2; missing evidence_pointer on a PROVED row fails; NOT_APPLICABLE in main array without ledger entry fails.
risk_if_missed: Without build-time gate, an Applicable HBR row can be silently shipped as PENDING; the registry becomes advisory rather than enforced.
validator_focus: Exit codes match documented contract; stderr is parseable JSONL; the gov-check umbrella actually invokes the sub-check (verify by failing a fixture and seeing umbrella fail).

minimum_controls: Failure path test: a packet with one PENDING HBR row must cause `just gov-check` (umbrella) to exit non-zero.
Stderr output must be JSONL (line-delimited) so CI pipelines can parse it.
Missing evidence_pointer on a PROVED row must fail (not silently warn).
